description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7 threat & injection cluster: whole-AI-system threat modeling vs single-defense design vs prompt-secrecy review vs disclosure controls, and against the shipped threat-modeler, security-pr-reviewer, and the ai-security-red-team-reviewer subagent.

cases.prompt: What could go wrong with our new LLM agent that reads wiki pages and files Jira tickets? Give me the full risk picture before we commit to the design.
cases.reason: A whole-system AI risk picture across all categories is this skill's core case; prompt-injection-defender designs one named defense, and threat-modeler lacks the AI-specific surface (the feature has an agent loop).

cases.prompt: Threat-model the new billing webhook endpoint — signature verification, replay, and idempotency concerns.
cases.reason: No model call, retrieval, or agent loop anywhere — classic STRIDE surface belongs to threat-modeler.

cases.prompt: Design our defense against prompt injection for the doc-summarizer — trust zones, how untrusted content is delimited, and what gates tool calls.
cases.reason: Designing the injection defense architecture itself is prompt-injection-defender; the threat modeler enumerates and routes that work, it doesn't do it.

cases.prompt: Security-review this PR that adds the OpenAI call and the new /summarize endpoint.
cases.reason: An actual diff gets the diff-review lens; the AI threat model is a design-time artifact, not a PR gate.

cases.prompt: Red-team our deployed chatbot: actually probe it for injection, jailbreaks, and data exfiltration and report what lands.
cases.reason: Hands-on adversarial probing of a live feature is the red-team subagent (composing ai-evaluation-harness for suites); the threat modeler predicts and maps, it does not probe.
